frameworks.name: mental simulation of internal models
frameworks.one_line: primates run model-based simulations of external dynamics to infer latent states

frameworks.name: recurrent neural network model of behavior
frameworks.one_line: RNNs with explicit dynamic-latent representation are compared to primate behavior

frameworks.name: RNN without dynamic latent tracking
frameworks.one_line: baseline networks lacking dynamic inference fail to match primate patterns

frameworks.name: behavioral modeling of physical inference
frameworks.one_line: prior work used behavioral models that do not emulate neural computations

core_question: Do primates rely on dynamic mental simulations of latent variables to make flexible physical predictions?
key_assumptions: primate physical inference involves latent-variable tracking
RNN dynamics can stand in for neural computations
ball interception requires inferring unobservable physical state
manipulations: presence vs absence of explicit dynamic-latent representation in RNNs
species comparison between humans and monkeys
training a large set of RNN variants on the interception task
key_findings: humans and monkeys show similar behavioral patterns on ball interception
RNNs with dynamic inference best capture the primate behavioral pattern
RNNs lacking dynamic latent tracking fail to match primate behavior
interpretations: the primate brain uses dynamic inferences to support flexible physical predictions
model neural systems can test computational hypotheses of higher brain function
